---
title: "Validation v4: Impact of the GD first-pass algorithm fix"
author: "Auto-generated validation report"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: show
    df_print: paged
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```

# 1. Introduction

This is the fourth validation report comparing **fixated** against
**analyzeeyedry** on `C1L1.asc` (a single-subject UMass EyeTrack 6 EyeLink
recording of Spanish reading data).

The primary goal of this report is to quantify the impact of the **GD
first-pass algorithm fix** implemented in `fixated` (see Section 2 for details).
In addition, this report demonstrates the new **`read_eyetrack_asc()`** function,
which replaces the hand-rolled `parse_c1l1()` helper used in v2 and v3.

The analysis uses the same parameter set as v3 (`shorttime = 80`,
`longtime = 800`, `ttcutoff = 4000`, `truncate_at_display_on = TRUE`).

---

# 2. The GD fix: what changed

**Before (v3 and earlier):** The GD first-pass loop treated *any* fixation
outside the current word as a first-pass terminator, regardless of duration.
This meant that a short (≤ `shorttime`) leftward or unassigned fixation would
terminate the scan, causing `fixated` to produce shorter GD values than
`analyzeeyedry` on trials where such fixations occurred.

**After (v4):** Only fixations whose `duration > shorttime` terminate the first
pass when they fall outside the word's boundaries.  Short fixations in any
direction are now skipped (not counted, not treated as terminators), matching
the eyedry/Robodoc convention.  The identical fix was applied to the `nfix`
counter.

The specific change in `R/compute_measures.R`:

```r
# Before — short leftward regression broke the first pass
if (past_l[i] && gd_NV > 0L) break

# After — only non-short leftward regressions break the first pass
if (past_l[i] && !is_sht[i] && gd_NV > 0L) break
```

---

# 3. Setup

```{r packages}
pkg_root          <- file.path(getwd(), "..")
analyzeeyedry_dir <- file.path(getwd(), "analyzeeyedry")

if (!requireNamespace("analyzeeyedry", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages(analyzeeyedry_dir, repos = NULL, type = "source")
}
if (!requireNamespace("fixated", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages(pkg_root, repos = NULL, type = "source")
}

library(analyzeeyedry)
library(fixated)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(knitr)

ASC_FILE <- file.path(pkg_root, "data_to_incorporate", "C1L1.asc")
stopifnot(file.exists(ASC_FILE))
cat("ASC file:", ASC_FILE, "\n")
cat("fixated version:", as.character(packageVersion("fixated")), "\n")
cat("analyzeeyedry version:", as.character(packageVersion("analyzeeyedry")), "\n")
```

---

# 4. Parse C1L1.asc with `read_eyetrack_asc()`

This section demonstrates the new `read_eyetrack_asc()` function, which
replaces the hand-rolled `parse_c1l1()` helper from v2/v3.

```{r parse-eyetrack}
et <- read_eyetrack_asc(ASC_FILE, trial_pattern = "^[A-Za-z]")
cat(sprintf("Parsed %d trials\n", nrow(et$trial_db)))
cat(sprintf("Fixations: %d rows\n", nrow(et$fixations)))
cat(sprintf("Word boundaries: %d rows across %d trials\n",
            nrow(et$word_boundaries),
            n_distinct(et$word_boundaries$trial_nr)))
```

```{r preview-trial-db}
head(et$trial_db, 5)
```

```{r preview-word-boundaries}
head(et$word_boundaries, 10)
```

---

# 5. analyzeeyedry reference analysis

```{r eyedry-analysis}
word_regions_edry <- asc_to_cnt(
  ASC_FILE, trial_pattern = "^[A-Za-z]",
  spillover_gap = 1L, spillover_size = 20L)
fix_edry   <- asc_to_fixations(ASC_FILE, subj = 1L, trial_pattern = "^[A-Za-z]")
fix_edry_r <- assign_regions_to_fixations(fix_edry, word_regions_edry)
nregmax_val <- max(word_regions_edry$nreg, na.rm = TRUE)

measures_edry <- compute_fixation_measures(
  fix_edry_r, word_regions_edry,
  shorttime = 80L, longtime = 800L, nregmax = nregmax_val)
cat(sprintf("analyzeeyedry: measures for %d trials\n", nrow(measures_edry)))
```

---

# 6. fixated analysis (v4: GD fix + `read_eyetrack_asc()`)

```{r fixated-v4}
measures_fix_v4 <- compute_eye_measures(
  et$fixations,
  et$word_boundaries,
  trial_col = "trial_nr",
  trial_db  = et$trial_db,
  truncate_at_display_on = TRUE,
  shorttime = 80L, longtime = 800L, ttcutoff = 4000L)
cat("fixated v4: measures computed for",
    n_distinct(measures_fix_v4$trial_nr), "trials\n")
```

---

# 7. Reshape analyzeeyedry output

```{r reshape-edry}
trial_key <- dplyr::tibble(
  trial_nr = seq_len(nrow(et$trial_db)),
  cond     = et$trial_db$cond,
  item     = et$trial_db$item)

pivot_edry_long <- function(wide_df, trial_key,
                             measures = c("ff","fp","tt","gp","oreg","ireg","pfix","nfix","sp")) {
  meas_pat  <- paste0("^(", paste(measures, collapse="|"), ")_R\\d+$")
  meas_cols <- names(wide_df)[grepl(meas_pat, names(wide_df))]
  long <- wide_df |>
    tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = dplyr::all_of(meas_cols),
                        names_to  = c("measure","word_id"),
                        names_pattern = "^(.+)_R(\\d+)$",
                        values_to = "value") |>
    dplyr::mutate(word_id = as.integer(word_id)) |>
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = measure, values_from = value) |>
    dplyr::rename(ffd=ff, gd=fp, tvt=tt, gpt=gp) |>
    dplyr::left_join(trial_key, by = c("cond", "item"))
  long
}

edry_long <- pivot_edry_long(measures_edry, trial_key)
cat("analyzeeyedry long format:", nrow(edry_long), "rows\n")
```

---

# 8. Merge and compare

```{r merge}
compare_v4 <- edry_long |>
  dplyr::select(trial_nr, cond, item, word_id,
                ffd_edry=ffd, gd_edry=gd, gpt_edry=gpt, tvt_edry=tvt,
                oreg_edry=oreg, ireg_edry=ireg, pfix_edry=pfix,
                nfix_edry=nfix, sp_edry=sp) |>
  dplyr::inner_join(
    measures_fix_v4 |>
      dplyr::select(trial_nr, word_id,
                    ffd_fix=ffd, gd_fix=gd, gpt_fix=gpt, tvt_fix=tvt,
                    oreg_fix=oreg, ireg_fix=ireg, pfix_fix=pfix,
                    nfix_fix=nfix, sp_fix=sp),
    by = c("trial_nr","word_id")) |>
  dplyr::arrange(trial_nr, word_id)

cat(sprintf("Comparison table: %d rows\n", nrow(compare_v4)))
```

---

# 9. Agreement analysis

## 9.1 Summary statistics

```{r summary-stats}
full_summary <- function(df, col_edry, col_fix, label) {
  d    <- df[[col_fix]] - df[[col_edry]]
  ref  <- pmax(abs(df[[col_edry]]), 1)
  re   <- abs(d) / ref
  both <- !is.na(df[[col_edry]]) & !is.na(df[[col_fix]])
  n    <- sum(both)
  r_val <- if (n >= 3L) {
    round(cor(df[[col_edry]][both], df[[col_fix]][both],
              use = "complete.obs"), 3)
  } else NA_real_
  data.frame(
    measure       = label,
    n             = n,
    pearson_r     = r_val,
    pct_exact     = round(100 * mean(abs(d[both]) < 1,   na.rm=TRUE), 1),
    pct_1pct      = round(100 * mean(re[both] <= 0.01,   na.rm=TRUE), 1),
    pct_5pct      = round(100 * mean(re[both] <= 0.05,   na.rm=TRUE), 1),
    pct_10pct     = round(100 * mean(re[both] <= 0.10,   na.rm=TRUE), 1),
    mean_abs_diff = round(mean(abs(d[both]), na.rm=TRUE), 1),
    median_abs_diff = round(median(abs(d[both]), na.rm=TRUE), 1),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

summary_tbl_v4 <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  full_summary(compare_v4, "ffd_edry", "ffd_fix", "FFD"),
  full_summary(compare_v4, "gd_edry",  "gd_fix",  "GD"),
  full_summary(compare_v4, "gpt_edry", "gpt_fix", "GPT"),
  full_summary(compare_v4, "tvt_edry", "tvt_fix", "TVT"),
  full_summary(compare_v4, "sp_edry",  "sp_fix",  "SP")
)
knitr::kable(summary_tbl_v4,
             col.names = c("Measure","N","r",
                           "% exact","% ≤1%","% ≤5%","% ≤10%",
                           "Mean |diff| (ms)","Median |diff| (ms)"),
             caption = "v4 (GD fix): agreement between fixated and analyzeeyedry.")
```

## 9.2 Scatter plots

```{r scatter-plots, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
scatter_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  compare_v4 |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(ffd_edry), !is.na(ffd_fix)) |>
    dplyr::transmute(measure = "FFD", edry = ffd_edry, fix = ffd_fix),
  compare_v4 |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(gd_edry),  !is.na(gd_fix))  |>
    dplyr::transmute(measure = "GD",  edry = gd_edry,  fix = gd_fix),
  compare_v4 |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(gpt_edry), !is.na(gpt_fix)) |>
    dplyr::transmute(measure = "GPT", edry = gpt_edry, fix = gpt_fix),
  compare_v4 |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(tvt_edry), !is.na(tvt_fix)) |>
    dplyr::transmute(measure = "TVT", edry = tvt_edry, fix = tvt_fix),
  compare_v4 |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(sp_edry),  !is.na(sp_fix))  |>
    dplyr::transmute(measure = "SP",  edry = sp_edry,  fix = sp_fix)
)

ggplot(scatter_df, aes(x = edry, y = fix)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.35, size = 0.7, colour = "#2166ac") +
  facet_wrap(~measure, scales = "free") +
  labs(title = "analyzeeyedry vs fixated v4 (GD fix): all continuous measures",
       x = "analyzeeyedry value (ms)",
       y = "fixated value (ms)") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11)
```

## 9.3 Discrete measures cross-tabulations

```{r discrete-measures}
for (meas in c("oreg", "ireg", "pfix", "nfix")) {
  ec <- paste0(meas, "_edry"); fc <- paste0(meas, "_fix")
  both <- compare_v4 |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data[[ec]]) | !is.na(.data[[fc]]))
  cat(sprintf("\n--- %s ---\n", toupper(meas)))
  ct <- table(edry = as.character(both[[ec]]),
              fixated = as.character(both[[fc]]),
              useNA = "ifany")
  print(ct)
  n_both  <- sum(!is.na(both[[ec]]) & !is.na(both[[fc]]))
  n_match <- sum(both[[ec]] == both[[fc]], na.rm = TRUE)
  cat(sprintf("Exact match: %d / %d (%.1f%%)\n",
              n_match, n_both, 100 * n_match / max(n_both, 1)))
}
```

## 9.4 Distribution of absolute differences

```{r diff-distributions, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
diff_long <- compare_v4 |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    FFD = abs(ffd_fix - ffd_edry),
    GD  = abs(gd_fix  - gd_edry),
    GPT = abs(gpt_fix - gpt_edry),
    TVT = abs(tvt_fix - tvt_edry),
    SP  = abs(sp_fix  - sp_edry)
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(FFD, GD, GPT, TVT, SP) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "measure",
                       values_to = "abs_diff") |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(abs_diff))

ggplot(diff_long, aes(x = abs_diff)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 60, fill = "#2166ac", colour = "white", alpha = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~measure, scales = "free") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", colour = "red",
             linewidth = 0.6) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of |fixated − analyzeeyedry| (v4, GD fix)",
       subtitle = "Red dashed line = 1 ms threshold",
       x = "Absolute difference (ms)", y = "Count") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11)
```

---

# 10. Remaining sources of disagreement

After applying the GD first-pass fix, the following residual causes of
disagreement are expected.

## 10.1 Fixation-to-word assignment near ROI boundaries

`analyzeeyedry` assigns each fixation to the character whose right boundary
first exceeds the fixation's x-coordinate (0-indexed character space).
`fixated` uses pixel-space ROI bounding boxes derived from the same
`REGION CHAR` messages but builds them as closed intervals.  Any space
character is included in the *preceding* word's ROI by the
`.build_word_rois_from_chars()` helper in `read_eyetrack_asc()`.  Remaining
discrepancies at boundaries will produce isolated per-trial offsets in FFD
and GD for words adjacent to spaces.

**Residual impact:** Small number of fixations (typically < 5 per sentence)
assigned to a different word.  Causes sporadic offsets of exactly one fixation
duration (~80–200 ms) in affected trials.

## 10.2 GPT accumulation with unassigned fixations

Both `analyzeeyedry` and `fixated` accumulate fixations during the go-past
window.  Small differences can arise when a fixation falls exactly on a
boundary pixel — one system may include it in the region and the other not.

**Residual impact:** Sporadic single-fixation GPT differences, typically
< 100 ms per affected trial.

## 10.3 `longtime` / `ttcutoff` borderline cases

When a fixation duration is exactly equal to `longtime` (800 ms), or when
cumulative TVT is exactly at `ttcutoff` (4000 ms), floating-point comparison
may differ between R's integer arithmetic and the compiled eyedry code.

**Residual impact:** Rare (< 1 % of cases); causes one system to return NA
while the other returns a numeric value.

## 10.4 Display-onset truncation rounding

When `truncate_at_display_on = TRUE`, a straddling fixation has its duration
recomputed as `end_time − t_display_on`.  The `analyzeeyedry` code uses
floating-point arithmetic for this calculation, which may differ from
`fixated`'s integer subtraction by up to 1 ms.

**Residual impact:** Up to 1 ms offset in FFD/GD for the first fixation of
each trial that straddles the display onset.

## 10.5 Conclusion

The GD first-pass fix (this report) is expected to be the most impactful
single change, substantially improving GD and consequently GPT, SP, nfix,
oreg, and ireg accuracy.  The remaining sources of disagreement are
intrinsic to differences between the two parsing strategies and are unlikely
to exceed 1–2 ms on average.  Further improvement would require adopting the
exact eyedry character-level region assignment algorithm in `fixated`.

---

```{r session-info}
sessionInfo()
```
